channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: State public-option health plans expand but can't fill gaps left by federal changes
item.description: Nevada recently became the third state to offer one of the plans on the ACA marketplaces. They're intended to be a cheaper insurance option but so far...

item.title: Sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitors may improve left ventricular diastolic function in hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
item.description: 1. Sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitors (SGLT-2i) were associated with improved left ventricular diastolic function and New York Heart Associatio...

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg to face jury in landmark social media addiction trial
item.description: The case is seen as a test of social media's legal responsibility for platform design features that plaintiffs' lawyers say exacerbated mental health ...

item.title: 8 creative ways to build your village, according to our listeners
item.description: NPR listeners share how they've made relationships with their neighbors and community. Many of them, through parties, potlucks and coffees, say they'v...

item.title: Brainwaves of mothers and children synchronize when playing together—even in an acquired language
item.description: Interbrain synchrony is the simultaneous activity of neural networks across the brains of people who are socially interacting—for example, talking, le...

item.title: Antimicrobial resistance in foodborne bacteria remains a public health concern in Europe
item.description: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) in common foodborne bacteria such as Salmonella and Campylobacter continues to be a public health concern across Europe...

item.title: Infliximab, Upadacitinib: Rapid Relief in Ulcerative Colitis
item.description: Analysis of 11 trials shows that some advanced treatments relieve symptoms faster and produce better short-term responses in severe ulcerative colitis...

item.title: Allergic conjunctivitis linked to increased risk for keratoconus
item.description: For patients aged 5 to 25 years, allergic conjunctivitis is associated with an increased risk for keratoconus and increased corneal diagnostic procedu...

item.title: C-reactive protein links to ventricular repolarization in coronary artery disease, study finds
item.description: A new study reveals that the predictive power of key inflammatory markers, such as C-reactive protein, shifts fundamentally depending on whether a pat...

item.title: A Single Dose of Short-Acting Psychedelic Rapidly Eases MDD
item.description: Dimethyltryptamine’s short duration of psychedelic effects makes it a potentially practical alternative to other longer-acting psychedelic therapies s...

item.title: Breast Cancer Patients Living Longer Post-Medicaid Expansion
item.description: Patients with breast cancer living in states with expanded Medicaid eligibility, have seen survival gains, raising more concerns about the impact of r...

item.title: Europe Groups Face Gaza’s Ongoing Healthcare Crisis
item.description: While the unmet needs of patients in Gaza are immense, new Israeli government requirements and other restrictions are challenging humanitarian organiz...

item.title: Home care: The Dutch model that challenges bureaucracy
item.description: Bureaucracy once swallowed Dutch home care. Buurtzorg flipped the script by trusting nurses and focusing on purpose.

item.title: Fibromyalgia-PTSD Link Shows Bidirectional Relationship
item.description: Research suggests a bidirectional relationship between posttraumatic stress disorder and fibromyalgia in military service members exposed to combat en...

item.title: This behavior can be a turning point for families caring for a dementia patient
item.description: Wandering, and "elopement," are frightening and dangerous behaviors by people with dementia. For some families, it is the point when they begin to see...

item.title: One in Six Medicare Beneficiaries Use Telehealth for Essential Care
item.description: 1. In this cross-sectional study, 16.4% of Medicare beneficiaries had at least one telehealth visit between 2021 and 2023, with users more likely to h...

item.title: A safe space for girls who've faced sexual violence
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is seeing a significant increase in acts of sexual violence against girls and young women. A support center offers a ...

item.title: Exposure to air pollution is associated with increased risk of developing limitations in physical function
item.description: 1. Greater long-term exposure to particulate matter (PM) and nitrogen dioxide (NO2) was associated with increased risk of progressing from no disabili...

item.title: 2MM: AI Roundup- Medtronic’s AI-Robotic Spine Platform, 90% Phase I Success for AI-Designed Drugs, and UCSD’s AI Cancer Risk Predictor [Feb 17th, 2026]

item.title: Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists may reduce endometrial cancer risk in endometrial hyperplasia
item.description: 1. Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists (GLP-1RA) in combination with progestins are significantly more effective at reducing endometrial cancer ...

item.title: The healing power of physician presence in modern medicine
item.description: I remember a patient who sat silently, staring at the hospital ceiling. She had survived a sudden illness that left her body weak and her spirit shake...

item.title: Deprescribing in health care: Why less medication can be more
item.description: Every day, millions of Americans take medications to manage chronic conditions or prevent disease. Prescription drugs are vital tools in modern medici...

item.title: Value-based care data gap: Why metrics fail to reach the bedside
item.description: Value-based care represents one of health care’s most important transformations, driving hospitals and health systems to focus on outcomes that truly ...

item.title: What the folinic acid retraction means for autism treatment
item.description: On January 29, 2026, the European Journal of Pediatrics formally retracted what had been the largest randomized clinical trial examining folinic acid ...

item.title: The pause medicine never taught us to take
item.description: As physicians, most of us do not need another Zoom webinar on burnout; we need open space. Even at the earliest stages of training, we are made to fee...
